UserGuide.Title: === Camera Settings Saver - User Guide ===
UserGuide.Section1Save:
Save Settings:
- Select one or more cameras from the Cameras tab
- Click 'Save Settings' to export current settings to JSON file
- Settings include brightness, contrast, white balance, etc.
UserGuide.Section1Apply:
Apply Settings:
- Select cameras you want to configure
- Click 'Apply Settings' to import settings from JSON file
- The app will match cameras by name or device path
UserGuide.Section2Content:
Default settings file: web_conf.json
You can:
- Type a custom filename
- Use 'Browse...' to select existing file
UserGuide.Section3Content:
All operations are logged in the 'Application Logs' tab
- Logs can be cleared, copied or saved to file
- Green text indicates successful operations
UserGuide.Section4Content:
The 'Service Control' tab allows you to manage the Windows service:

INSTALLATION:
- Configure service parameters (profile path, language, interval, log file)
- Click 'Install Service' to register the Windows service
- Service will start automatically on system boot

OPERATION:
- 'Start Service' - manually start the service
- 'Stop Service' - manually stop the service
- 'Refresh Status' - update current service status
- 'Uninstall Service' - remove the service from Windows

REQUIREMENTS:
- Administrator privileges required for all service operations
- Service executable location: bin\CameraSettingsService.exe
UserGuide.Section5Content:
The service accepts the following command line arguments:

/profile "path\to\file.json"   - Configuration file path (default: web_conf.json)
/lang {en|ru}                    - Language for logging (default: en)
/interval N                       - Execution interval in seconds (default: 20)
/log "path\to\service.log"       - Log file path (optional)

Example:
  sc create CameraSettingsService binPath= "C:\Program Files\CameraSettings\bin\CameraSettingsService.exe /p web_conf.json" start= auto
UserGuide.Section6Content:
- If a camera isn't listed, click 'Refresh List'
- Some cameras may not support all settings
- Check logs for detailed error information
- For service issues, check Windows Event Viewer
- Ensure you're running as Administrator for service operations
UserGuide.QuickStart5: 4. Load existing settings and click 'Apply Settings'
UserGuide.QuickStart6: 5. Go to Service tab to install and manage Windows service
UserGuide.ConsoleHelpFileFormatText: Settings are saved/loaded in JSON format.

Service.Warnings.IntervalMustBePositive: Interval must be positive. Using default (20 seconds)
Service.Warnings.InvalidInterval: Invalid interval format. Using default (20 seconds)

ServiceHelp.Title: Camera Settings Service Help
ServiceHelp.Description: Windows service for automatically applying camera settings from configuration file.
ServiceHelp.OptionInterval: /interval, /i N       - Execution interval in seconds (default: 20, min: 1, max: 3600)
ServiceHelp.OptionLog: /log, /l [FILE]        - Enable logging to file (optional filename, default: service.log)
ServiceHelp.InstallNote: NOTE: This is a Windows service. It should be installed via SC command or the WebCamSettings application.
ServiceHelp.ScInstall: sc create CameraSettingsService binPath= "\"C:\Program Files\CameraSettings\bin\CameraSettingsService.exe\" /p web_conf.json" start= auto
ServiceHelp.EasierWay: EASIER WAY: Use the WebCamSettings application (Service tab) to install, start, stop and uninstall the service with a graphical interface.
